title: Urban Campsites - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Urban Campsites 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITY OF ASHLAND DUSK TO DAWN OVERNIGHT SLEEPING DUSK TO DAWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a temporary overnight camping space. Provides overnight guests with coffee, breakfast, snacks, hygiene and first aid supplies. Limited storage of guests' belongings limited to one container and based on availability. 1175 E Main Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness. Pets allowed but must be under their owners control at all times. Hours: 7 days per week 7pm-7:30am Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Snacks: 7 days per week 7pm Email (541) 488-6002 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO SHORT-TERM SLEEPING AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a tented area for individuals to sleep. Basic sleeping materials are available for those who do not have them. Guests may stay for 12 hours and may return after a 12-hour break. Also offers a clothing closet. Item availability depends on what has been donated and is on hand at any given time. 999 N Thunderbird Way Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with exceptions for legally emancipated teens Hours: Line up and sign up at 5pm. Check in at 9pm. 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (closed Sunday 7am-8pm) Clothing closet: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 10am-4pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE RETREAT CROSSINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Urban campground with a 150-bed capacity. The facility operates through three distinct phases: tents, non-congregate shelter, and a village. In Phase 1, residents are provided tent sites and meals. The focus here is on addressing urgent needs. Participants may not use drugs or alcohol on-site but there are no sobriety requirements for participants to receive services. Phase 2 transitions residents to non-congregate shelters, offering more privacy and security. Residents have access to Pallet Shelters and Rigid Tents which have electrical outlets, heating and cooling, and a locking door. There is a shower and laundry trailer. Residents in this phase are required to participate in case managment services. They also pay a monthly fee and are responsible for their own meals. Phase 3 involves moving into a village setting, where residents live in small, individual units and are required to have 30 days clean and sober. The village environment encourages community building and peer support. 711 E Main Street Suite 25 Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals and couples age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Apply in person or call: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 499-0880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION DAWN TO DAWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a place for unsheltered individuals to sleep safely overnight in heated, heavy-duty communal tents. The shelter has a capacity for 125 guests with a low-barrier entry and no time limit for guests who follow the shelter guidelines. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless individuals ages 18 and older Hours: Bed Assignment: 2pm ESS is open 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 689-5820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF ASHLAND DUSK TO DAWN RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO SHORT-TERM SLEEPING AREA ROGUE RETREAT CROSSINGS ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY DAWN TO DAWN
